assumptions: A terminal with cURL is available.

developer_goal: Create a hosted Supabase project, expose a leaderboard table via Data API, and query it with cURL.

first_success.outcome: cURL GET /rest/v1/leaderboard returns JSON rows ordered by score.
first_success.observable_signal: Successful JSON array including inserted players (alice/bob/carol/dave) or equivalent rows.

route_selection.surface: Hosted Supabase project + cURL Data API leaderboard
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: Hosted Supabase project + cURL Data API leaderboard

candidates.name: Hosted Supabase project + cURL Data API leaderboard
candidates.reason: API quickstart builds a public leaderboard table and documents cURL GET to https://<PROJECT_REF>.supabase.co/rest/v1/leaderboard with apikey header. Prefer hosted project + cURL over framework quickstarts.

required_path.action: Create a Supabase account and a new project; wait until the project is ready.
required_path.observable_result: Table Editor and API settings available.

required_path.action: Create public.leaderboard (SQL or Table Editor) with player and score columns.
Expose the table to the Data API / anon role and configure RLS select policy for public reads as documented.
required_path.observable_result: Anon can select per quickstart grants/policies.

prerequisites.description: Publishable/anon API key from project API settings.

sources.title: Build an API route in less than 2 minutes

uncertainties.question: Are all signup and first-success fields for Supabase fully enumerated in public docs?
uncertainties.impact: Atomic field counts inside account or billing steps may be incomplete.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Live console fields can differ from documentation screenshots.
